title: EDHOC test accesses private Context fields
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs
Lines: 1032-1045

The test_full_handshake test tries to access private fields of Context struct:
- initiator_ctx.sender_key
- responder_ctx.recipient_key
- initiator_ctx.recipient_key
- responder_ctx.sender_key
- initiator_ctx.common_iv
- responder_ctx.common_iv

These fields are private (no pub modifier in lib.rs:165-188). The edhoc module is a sibling module, not a descendant, so it cannot access private fields of Context defined in the parent crate module.

This test should fail to compile. Either:
1. The test is dead code that never runs
2. There's a visibility issue that needs fixing
3. Public accessors need to be added for testing

To fix: Either add pub(crate) test accessors to Context, or restructure the test to use public APIs only (e.g., test roundtrip encryption/decryption instead of comparing raw keys).
close_reason: Fixed: Replaced direct private field access (sender_key, recipient_key, common_iv) with functional roundtrip test. Test now verifies EDHOC-derived OSCORE contexts work by encrypting/decrypting requests and responses between initiator and responder, which is more robust than comparing raw keys and avoids relying on implementation details.
